from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about artists (or related entities) into a fluent description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    def _clean(value: str) -> str:
        """Strip surrounding quotes and whitespace."""
        return value.strip().strip('"').strip("'")

    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language fragments.
    # The placeholder {obj} will be replaced by the cleaned object string.
    pred_map = {
        "birthDate": "was born on {obj}",
        "birthYear": "was born in {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {obj}",
        "origin": "originates from {obj}",
        "genre": "plays {obj}",
        "instrument": "plays {obj}",
        "occupation": "is a {obj}",
        "nationality": "is {obj}",
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": "is associated with {obj}",
        "recordLabel": "is signed to {obj}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "started performing in {obj}",
        "activeYearsEndYear": "ended performing in {obj}",
        "deathDate": "died on {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "alternativeName": "is also known as {obj}",
        "stylisticOrigin": "originated from {obj}",
        "musicFusionGenre": "fused with {obj}",
        "musicSubgenre": "is a subgenre of {obj}",
        "derivative": "has a derivative {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "governingBody": "is governed by {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "country": "belongs to the country {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "parentCompany": "is owned by {obj}",
        "bandMember": "has band member {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "anthem": "has anthem {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the currency {obj}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "distributingCompany": "is distributed by {obj}",
        "training": "was trained at {obj}",
        "populationDensity": "has a population density of {obj}",
        "meaning": "means \"{obj}\"",
        "longName": "full name is \"{obj}\"",
        "areaTotal": "covers an area of {obj}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies at {obj} meters above sea level",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {obj}",
        "background": "has background as a {obj}",
    }

    # Group clauses by their subject to allow multiple subjects in the input.
    # Aggregate objects per subject and predicate to allow natural list joining.
    def _join_items(items: list[str]) -> str:
        """Join a list of strings with commas and 'and' before the last item."""
        if not items:
            return ""
        if len(items) == 1:
            return items[0]
        return ", ".join(items[:-1]) + " and " + items[-1]

    # subject -> predicate -> list of objects
    subject_pred_objs: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subj = t.subject
        pred = t.predicate
        obj = _clean(t.object)
        subject_pred_objs.setdefault(subj, {}).setdefault(pred, []).append(obj)

    # Pre‑compute clause strings for each subject (without the leading subject name)
    subject_clause_text: dict[str, str] = {}
    for subj, pred_objs in subject_pred_objs.items():
        parts: list[str] = []
        for pred, objs in pred_objs.items():
            template = pred_map.get(pred, f"{pred} {{obj}}")
            joined_obj = _join_items(objs)
            clause = template.format(obj=joined_obj) if "{obj}" in template else template
            parts.append(clause)
        # Join parts for the subject using commas and an 'and' before the last part
        if len(parts) == 1:
            subject_clause_text[subj] = parts[0]
        else:
            subject_clause_text[subj] = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1]

    # Build the main sentence using the first subject as the primary focus.
    # Embed information about other subjects when they appear as objects.
    primary_subj = next(iter(subject_pred_objs))
    primary_parts: list[str] = []
    for pred, objs in subject_pred_objs[primary_subj].items():
        template = pred_map.get(pred, f"{pred} {{obj}}")
        processed_objs: list[str] = []
        for obj in objs:
            if obj in subject_clause_text:
                # Embed the related subject's clause as a relative clause.
                rel_clause = subject_clause_text[obj]
                # Ensure the relative clause starts with a lower‑case verb if needed.
                if rel_clause and rel_clause[0].isupper():
                    rel_clause = rel_clause[0].lower() + rel_clause[1:]
                processed_objs.append(f"{obj}, which {rel_clause}")
            else:
                processed_objs.append(obj)
        joined_obj = _join_items(processed_objs)
        clause = template.format(obj=joined_obj) if "{obj}" in template else template
        primary_parts.append(clause)

    # Assemble the final sentence.
    if len(primary_parts) == 1:
        body = primary_parts[0]
    else:
        body = ", ".join(primary_parts[:-1]) + ", and " + primary_parts[-1]

    sentence = f"{primary_subj} {body}."
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
